key_insight: Combinatorial optimization provides a unified way to make machine learning systems more trustworthy by enabling formal reasoning about interpretability, robustness, fairness, privacy, and certification.
engineering_relevance: For deployed ML systems, CO-based methods can complement heuristic and gradient-based approaches with global guarantees, explicit trade-off handling, and audit-friendly certificates.
method: Survey CO formulations for trustworthy ML across training and post-training tasks.
Covers interpretable model learning, explanation generation, robustness analysis, fairness auditing, compression, and privacy protection/attacks.
Highlights hybrid optimization and solver-based workflows as practical tools for certification and model selection.
evidence_signal: The paper is a survey that synthesizes recent advances across multiple trustworthy-ML tasks, emphasizing optimization- and certification-oriented reasoning rather than reporting a single benchmark study.
watch_item: Scalability remains a key limitation, so CO methods may be difficult to apply directly to large modern models without hybrid approximations or stronger solvers.
paper_title: Trustworthy Machine Learning through the Lens of Combinatorial Optimization: Survey and Research Perspectives
